message:
perf(fcp-crypto,fcp-mesh): reduce allocations in CWT claims, Shamir reconstruct, and gossip signing

Three allocation-reduction improvements in hot cryptographic and gossip paths:

fcp-crypto/cose.rs:
- CwtClaims::operations(): Replace iterator .map().collect() with
  pre-allocated Vec::with_capacity(ops.len()) and explicit push loop.
  Avoids the iterator adapter overhead and ensures a single allocation
  for the exact number of operations.
- CwtClaims::grant_objects(): Same pattern — pre-allocate and push
  instead of map/collect.
- CwtClaims::to_cbor(): Pre-allocate Vec::with_capacity(256) instead
  of Vec::new() for CBOR serialization buffer. Typical CWT claims
  serialize to 200-300 bytes, so this avoids 2-3 reallocations during
  ciborium's incremental writes.

fcp-crypto/shamir.rs:
- reconstruct_secret(): Hoist the GF(256) points buffer outside the
  per-byte loop. Previously allocated a new Vec<(Gf256, Gf256)> for
  every byte of the secret via map/collect. Now reuses a single
  Vec::with_capacity(shares.len()) buffer, calling .clear() each
  iteration. For a 32-byte secret with 5 shares, this eliminates 31
  unnecessary allocations.

fcp-mesh/gossip.rs:
- GossipSummary::signing_bytes(): Pre-allocate Vec::with_capacity()
  using an estimated size (152 bytes fixed overhead + IBLT length)
  instead of Vec::new(). Gossip signing happens on every round, so
  avoiding 3-4 reallocations per summary is meaningful under load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
